Title: The tomato OST1-VOZ1 module regulates drought-mediated flowering

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: SLOST1 is also referred to as S/OST1 or SIOST1 in the text and is highly similar (>85% identity) to Arabidopsis OST1/SnRK2.6.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. SLOST1 is a tomato SnRK2/OST1 kinase highly similar to Arabidopsis OST1 and is expressed in multiple tissues, including leaves, buds, and fruits.
2. CRISPR/Cas9 loss-of-function slost1 mutants are hypersensitive to drought and osmotic stress, show higher water loss rates and lower leaf temperature, and are delayed in flowering under both normal and drought conditions.
3. SLOST1 directly interacts with and phosphorylates the transcription factor SIVOZ1 at Ser67, enhancing SIVOZ1 protein stability and nuclear accumulation in an ABA-dependent manner.
4. Through SIVOZ1, SLOST1 positively regulates expression of the flowering integrator SFT and is essential for full drought-accelerated flowering in tomato.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Phosphoproteomics screen to identify SLOST1 substrates including SIVOZ1; DAP-seq data (NCBI PRJNA734096) used indirectly through SIVOZ1 to define downstream targets such as SFT.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function slost1 mutants are hypersensitive to drought and exhibit delayed flowering under both normal and drought conditions, indicating that SLOST1 positively regulates drought tolerance and flowering transition, including drought-accelerated flowering.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Upon ABA and drought, SLOST1 kinase activity is activated and it directly phosphorylates SIVOZ1 at Ser67, which enhances SIVOZ1 protein stability, reduces proteasomal degradation, and promotes its nuclear accumulation, thereby enabling SIVOZ1-dependent transcriptional activation of downstream targets such as SFT.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA-activated SnRK2/OST1 signaling pathway that integrates drought stress perception with flowering control via phosphorylation-dependent activation of SIVOZ1 and subsequent induction of the flowering integrator SFT.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: SLOST1 --(phosphorylation/activation)--> SIVOZ1; SLOST1 --(indirect positive regulation via SIVOZ1)--> SFT.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SnRK2 family serine/threonine protein kinase OST1 ortholog
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mainly in cytoplasm and nucleus in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf cells; interacts with SIVOZ1 in cytoplasm and partly in nucleus
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SIVOZ1 (demonstrated by split-luciferase complementation, co-immunoprecipitation in Arabidopsis protoplasts, and BiFC in N. benthamiana).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in leaves, buds, and fruits at different developmental stages; kinase activity activated by ABA
Plant_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: CRISPR/Cas9-induced deletions in the SLOST1 coding region: 22-bp and 220-bp deletions in exon 1 in the Micro Tom background and 2-bp and 5-bp deletions in the Ailsa Craig background; position: genic; reported impact: affects_function (loss-of-function with reduced drought tolerance and delayed flowering).
Plant_Genes.Key_Variant_Type: Indel (CRISPR/Cas9-generated deletions in the coding sequence).
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil drought stress (water withholding and controlled low soil water content), osmotic stress induced by 200 mM mannitol, and ABA treatment, all of which modulate SLOST1 activity and associated phenotypes.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: ABA-mediated drought tolerance and flowering time regulation, including drought-accelerated flowering, in tomato
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought tolerance, ABA response); flowering time / flowering transition; drought escape (drought-accelerated flowering)
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: slost1 mutants show higher water loss rate of detached leaves, lower leaf surface temperature consistent with more open stomata, hypersensitivity to mannitol-induced osmotic stress, ABA-insensitive seed germination, and reduced overall plant size compared with wild type.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: CRISPR-Cas9-edited slost1 mutant lines (slost1-1 and slost1-2 in Micro Tom; slost1-3 and slost1-4 in Ailsa Craig) and their corresponding wild-type cultivars.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Cultivars Micro Tom (MT) and Ailsa Craig (AC; LA2838A)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: BLAST-based gene identification and phylogenetic analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing and mutant screening; drought survival and recovery assays; mannitol and ABA treatments; water loss rate measurements and thermal infrared imaging; in vitro kinase assays; Phos-Tag mobility-shift assays; split-luciferase complementation imaging; co-immunoprecipitation; BiFC; RT-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9-generated knockout alleles in two genetic backgrounds (Micro Tom and Ailsa Craig) combined with drought survival assays, water loss rate and leaf temperature measurements, ABA and mannitol treatments, and flowering time quantification under long- and short-day conditions; kinase activity assays, phosphoproteomics, protein–protein interaction (LCI, Co-IP, BiFC) and Phos-Tag phosphorylation assays to establish molecular function.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a tomato SnRK2 homolog by BLAST search of the tomato genome (ITAG2.4) using Arabidopsis SnRK2s; Solyc01g108280 showed high similarity to AtOST1 and was designated SLOST1/S/OST1.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because SLOST1 simultaneously enhances drought tolerance and promotes flowering, including drought-accelerated flowering, manipulating SLOST1 activity or expression could help develop tomato varieties with improved performance and yield stability under water-limited conditions by optimizing both stress resistance and flowering time.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: SIVOZ1 shares high sequence similarity with Arabidopsis VOZ1; the critical phosphorylation site Ser67 was functionally validated using a nonphosphorylatable SIVOZ1S67A mutant, which fails to gain stability or nuclear accumulation in response to SLOST1.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. SIVOZ1 is a VOZ1-type NAC transcription factor highly expressed in multiple tomato tissues and identified as a direct substrate of the kinase SLOST1.
2. SLOST1 phosphorylates SIVOZ1 at Ser67, which is essential for SIVOZ1 protein stability and nuclear localization and is enhanced by ABA treatment.
3. CRISPR/Cas9 slvoz1 mutants are late flowering, hypersensitive to drought/osmotic stress, and less responsive to ABA, indicating that SIVOZ1 positively regulates both drought tolerance and flowering transition.
4. DAP-seq, EMSA, transactivation, and ChIP-qPCR show that SIVOZ1 binds specific promoter motifs and directly activates the flowering integrator SFT, making SIVOZ1 a central regulator of drought-accelerated flowering in tomato.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Identified as a SLOST1 substrate by phosphoproteomics; genome-wide SIVOZ1 binding sites and target genes characterized by DAP-seq with reads aligned to the tomato reference genome (DAP-seq project PRJNA734096).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR slvoz1 mutants flower later than wild type under both long-day and short-day conditions and are hypersensitive to drought/osmotic stress and less responsive to drought-accelerated flowering, demonstrating that SIVOZ1 positively regulates drought tolerance and flowering transition in tomato.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Phosphorylation of SIVOZ1 at Ser67 by SLOST1 increases SIVOZ1 protein stability by reducing proteasome-dependent degradation and promotes its nuclear translocation in an ABA-dependent manner; in the nucleus, SIVOZ1 binds specific DNA motifs (including GC(G/T)T(N)7A(A/C)GC, GATGGCATGCCACGC, CGACCGTGTGTGC, TACGCNAC/T, and ABRE-like ACGTA/G motifs) in target promoters such as SFT to activate transcription.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Central transcription factor in an ABA-activated SLOST1–SIVOZ1–SFT module that links drought stress signaling to activation of flowering integrator genes and drought-accelerated flowering.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: SLOST1 --(phosphorylation/activation at Ser67)--> SIVOZ1; SIVOZ1 --(direct transcriptional activation via promoter binding)--> SFT.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: VOZ (VASCULAR PLANT ONE-ZINC FINGER) NAC-type transcription factor
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Primarily localized in the cytoplasm under control conditions; after ABA treatment and co-expression with SLOST1, SIVOZ1 accumulates in the nucleus in N. benthamiana and Arabidopsis cells; nuclear SIVOZ1 is reduced in slost1 mutants compared with wild type.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SLOST1 (direct interaction demonstrated by LCI, co-immunoprecipitation in Arabidopsis protoplasts, and BiFC in N. benthamiana).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in tomato leaves, roots, flower tissues, and fruits; SIVOZ1 protein levels are lower in slost1 mutants than in wild type in leaves, buds, and flowers, whereas SIVOZ1 transcript levels are similar, indicating posttranscriptional regulation.
Plant_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: CRISPR/Cas9-induced loss-of-function mutations in the SIVOZ1 coding sequence generating slvoz1-1, slvoz1-2, slvoz1-3, and slvoz1-4 alleles; position: genic; reported impact: affects_function (late flowering and reduced drought tolerance).
Plant_Genes.Key_Variant_Type: Indel (CRISPR/Cas9-generated mutations in the coding region).
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA and drought/osmotic stress (mannitol) treatments, which activate SLOST1 kinase activity and thereby enhance SIVOZ1 phosphorylation, protein stability, and nuclear accumulation.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Transcriptional regulation of drought tolerance and flowering time, including drought-accelerated flowering, downstream of ABA/SLOST1 signaling in tomato.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought tolerance, osmotic stress response, ABA sensitivity); flowering time / flowering transition; drought escape (drought-accelerated flowering).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: slvoz1 mutants show hypersensitivity to mannitol, faster water loss and lower leaf temperature than wild type, and reduced sensitivity to ABA during seed germination, indicating roles in ABA-mediated drought responses.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: CRISPR-Cas9-derived slvoz1 mutant lines (slvoz1-1 and slvoz1-2 in Micro Tom; slvoz1-3 and slvoz1-4 in Ailsa Craig) and their wild-type controls; SIVOZ1 overexpression constructs and SIVOZ1S67A mutant constructs for biochemical and localization assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Cultivars Micro Tom (MT) and Ailsa Craig (AC; LA2838A)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: CRISPR/Cas9 editing; drought and mannitol stress assays; water loss rate and thermal imaging; ABA sensitivity assays; in vitro kinase and Phos-Tag assays; cell-free degradation and protoplast protein stability assays; confocal microscopy for localization; cell fractionation; DAP-seq; EMSA; transient SFTpro:LUC transactivation; ChIP-qPCR; RT-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Generation of independent CRISPR/Cas9 slvoz1 mutant alleles in two tomato backgrounds followed by flowering time analysis under long- and short-day conditions and drought treatment; drought and osmotic tolerance assays (mannitol, water loss, leaf temperature, electrolyte leakage, ABA sensitivity); in vitro kinase assays and Phos-Tag assays to confirm SLOST1-dependent phosphorylation at Ser67; cell-free degradation and protoplast stability assays; subcellular localization and nuclear translocation assays; DAP-seq, EMSA, transactivation (SFTpro:LUC), and ChIP-qPCR to identify and validate direct DNA targets.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a putative SLOST1 substrate by phosphoproteomics analysis and as a VOZ1 homolog by sequence similarity to Arabidopsis VOZ1; the SIVOZ1 phosphorylation site Ser67 was mapped by phosphoproteomics.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a key transcription factor that promotes both drought tolerance and flowering, and directly activates SFT under drought, SIVOZ1 represents a promising target to engineer or select alleles that optimize drought-escape flowering and stress resilience in tomato, potentially improving yield under water-deficit conditions.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Previously known to play a dominant role in controlling flowering transition in day-neutral tomato; this study extends its role to drought-associated flowering by showing SIVOZ1-dependent transcriptional activation and stress-induced expression.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. SFT is an FT ortholog and major flowering-integrator gene in tomato.
2. DAP-seq and motif analysis reveal multiple SIVOZ1 binding motifs in the SFT promoter, and EMSA confirms direct binding of SIVOZ1 but not SLOST1 to these promoter fragments.
3. Transient SFTpro:LUC assays show that SIVOZ1 activates SFT transcription, with SLOST1 slightly enhancing this activation, and ChIP-qPCR confirms in vivo binding of SIVOZ1 to SFT promoter regions.
4. SFT transcript levels are induced by mannitol (drought-mimic) treatment in wild-type seedlings but remain low in slost1 and slvoz1 mutants, correlating reduced SFT expression with delayed flowering and attenuated drought-accelerated flowering.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq (NCBI PRJNA734096) used to identify SFT as a high-confidence SIVOZ1 target with promoter-localized binding peaks.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SFT functions as a major flowering integrator whose expression is induced by drought/ osmotic stress and is directly activated by SIVOZ1; reduced SFT expression in slost1 and slvoz1 mutants correlates with their delayed flowering and attenuated drought-accelerated flowering.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: SIVOZ1 binds directly to multiple motifs (including ABRE/G-box-related sites) in the SFT promoter and activates SFT transcription; SLOST1 slightly enhances SIVOZ1-mediated SFT transactivation by stabilizing and promoting nuclear accumulation of SIVOZ1.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts as the flowering-integrator component of the SLOST1–SIVOZ1–SFT pathway that mediates drought-accelerated flowering in tomato.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: SIVOZ1 --(direct transcriptional activation via promoter binding)--> SFT; SLOST1 --(indirect positive regulation via SIVOZ1 phosphorylation and stabilization)--> SFT.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Acts as a major flowering-integrator gene; SFT transcript levels are reduced in slost1 and slvoz1 mutants compared with wild type under normal conditions and are induced by mannitol (drought-mimicking) treatment in wild-type seedlings but remain relatively low in the mutants.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic/drought-mimicking stress imposed by mannitol, which induces SFT expression in wild-type seedlings and is used to probe drought-associated flowering responses.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Flowering time regulation and integration of drought signals downstream of SLOST1–SIVOZ1 signaling in tomato.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Flowering time / flowering transition; drought escape (drought-accelerated flowering).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Wild-type and slost1 and slvoz1 mutant tomato seedlings (Micro Tom background) for SFT expression analyses; recombinant SIVOZ1 protein and SFT promoter fragments for EMSA; tomato leaves for ChIP-qPCR; N. benthamiana leaves for SFTpro:LUC reporter assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Primarily analyzed at the transcript level in Micro Tom background seedlings and mutants (slost1 and slvoz1).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: DAP-seq; motif discovery; EMSA with FAM-labeled SFT promoter probes; transient SFTpro:LUC transactivation assays in N. benthamiana; ChIP-qPCR using anti-SIVOZ1 antibody; RT-qPCR of SFT in wild type and mutants under control and mannitol (drought-mimic) treatments.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq to identify SFT as a SIVOZ1-bound gene; motif analysis showing multiple SIVOZ1 binding motifs in the SFT promoter; EMSA demonstrating direct binding of SIVOZ1 (but not SLOST1) to SFT promoter fragments; transient SFTpro:LUC assays in N. benthamiana leaves showing SIVOZ1-dependent transcriptional activation; ChIP-qPCR using anti-SIVOZ1 antibody on tomato plants confirming in vivo binding of SIVOZ1 to SFT promoter regions; RT-qPCR showing SFT induction by mannitol and reduced SFT expression in slost1 and slvoz1 mutants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously characterized as a FLOWERING LOCUS T (FT) ortholog and major flowering integrator in tomato; in this study, SFT was identified as a direct SIVOZ1 target by DAP-seq and confirmed by biochemical and transcriptional assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a major flowering-integrator gene directly activated by SIVOZ1 under drought-related conditions, SFT is a key downstream node through which breeding or engineering of the SLOST1–SIVOZ1 module could fine-tune flowering time and drought-escape responses in tomato.
